title: Celebrity Pharmacology

jokes.text: Flying a little low? Maybe you should get high. - Who's this? - This is Drugs.
jokes.setup: Bee character tired and flying low, friend suggests getting high (altitude)
jokes.punchline: High means drugs, immediately introducing the drugs character
jokes.explanation: Clean double meaning on 'get high' works within the drug awareness context, economical setup

jokes.text: Drugs are like special honey. They give you a buzzzzz. Don't call me honey, honey.
jokes.setup: Drugs character using bee-themed metaphor about giving a buzz
jokes.punchline: Pierce's improvised misunderstanding of 'honey' as term of endearment
jokes.explanation: Pierce's confusion fits his character, layered wordplay with bee/buzz theme

jokes.text: Pierce! Drugs doesn't say that.
jokes.setup: Pierce improvising lines not in the script
jokes.punchline: Annie's frustrated correction, treating the character 'Drugs' like a real person
jokes.explanation: Annie's control-freak nature showing through, absurd seriousness about fictional character

jokes.text: I don't say anything, by the way. I mean, I thought this show was about drug awareness. Who's gonna be aware of a character that has no lines?
jokes.setup: Pierce playing a silent drugs character in an educational play
jokes.punchline: Logical complaint about the ineffectiveness of a silent antagonist
jokes.explanation: Pierce's vanity disguised as legitimate criticism, makes actual sense

jokes.text: I thought it made them just custom-paint their vans and solve mysteries.
jokes.setup: Discussion about marijuana making people work faster
jokes.punchline: Reference to Scooby-Doo stoner stereotype
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Scooby-Doo reference without naming it, economical and clever pop culture observation

jokes.text: Also, are you sure that my costume is on backwards? Somehow I felt more confident before.
jokes.setup: Troy questioning his costume orientation
jokes.punchline: Implication that wearing costume backwards affected his confidence
jokes.explanation: Troy's innocent confusion, visual element of backwards costume

jokes.text: I don't think my character would say 'tripping balls.' That's not a question. I don't think my character would say tripping balls, okay?
jokes.setup: Shirley objecting to crude drug terminology in the script
jokes.punchline: Her discomfort saying the phrase while repeatedly saying it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Shirley character moment, conservative values clashing with crude language

jokes.text: You don't count, Britta. You don't respond to anything appropriately. Thank you!
jokes.setup: Jeff dismissing Britta's opinion as inappropriate
jokes.punchline: Britta's enthusiastic 'Thank you!' proving his point
jokes.explanation: Britta immediately proving Jeff's point about inappropriate responses, perfect character consistency

jokes.text: Dean-dong! I'm just checking in to see how the Greendale antidrug players are doing.
jokes.setup: Dean making entrance
jokes.punchline: Ding-dong/Dean-dong wordplay
jokes.explanation: Simple pun fits Dean's quirky character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: So with any luck, we can get rid of all those Charleston Chews in the vending machines.
jokes.setup: Dean talking about drug awareness benefits
jokes.punchline: Non-sequitur about Charleston Chews as if they're dangerous
jokes.explanation: Absurd connection between drug awareness and candy, Dean's scattered priorities

jokes.text: But as you can see from my outfit, I already have plans, so off to the airport Ramada!
jokes.setup: Dean explaining why he can't participate
jokes.punchline: Reference to elaborate costume for hotel visit
jokes.explanation: Dean's costume obsession and mundane destination contrast

jokes.text: How come he gets a front stinger?
jokes.setup: Pierce watching Dean leave
jokes.punchline: Bee pun on 'front singer' while in bee costume
jokes.explanation: Decent bee-themed pun, Pierce's jealousy showing

jokes.text: Do you think bees eat their own honey? I'm sure they've at least tasted it.
jokes.setup: Troy's random philosophical bee question
jokes.punchline: Abed's logical but understated response
jokes.explanation: Classic Troy-Abed dynamic, Troy's random thought met with Abed's logic

jokes.text: That's like saying you don't work by electric light. If you want to have an active sex life, part of it is texting.
jokes.setup: Discussion about not liking text flirting
jokes.punchline: Comparing texting to electric light as modern necessity
jokes.explanation: Jeff's smooth confidence about modern dating, good analogy

jokes.text: Shirley, since I might be the father of your unborn child, I made you a mix tape. I hope you like Johnny Gill.
jokes.setup: Chang addressing paternity situation
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely romantic gesture with specific R&B artist
jokes.explanation: Chang's inappropriate romantic gesture, awkward specificity of Johnny Gill

jokes.text: Chang. I know who you're talking about... He doesn't exist!
jokes.setup: Britta claiming not to know who they're talking about
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting herself then denying his existence
jokes.explanation: Britta's denial strategy backfiring immediately, psychological defense mechanism

jokes.text: I was kind of the Gerber Baby of moist towelettes.
jokes.setup: Pierce explaining his commercial background
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and unsexy product comparison to iconic baby
jokes.explanation: Brilliant specificity, perfect Pierce vanity about unsexy product, highly quotable

jokes.text: I do physical comedy. Funny dances. Foreign accents. Name any country. Russia.
jokes.setup: Pierce listing his acting abilities, challenging Annie to name a country
jokes.punchline: Pierce immediately naming Russia himself
jokes.explanation: Pierce's ego preventing him from waiting for response, self-defeating showcase

jokes.text: Right. Uh... is the message Jeff Winger? Because he's on every page of this thing.
jokes.setup: Annie saying the message is the star of the show
jokes.punchline: Pierce pointing out Jeff's prominent role instead
jokes.explanation: Pierce's jealousy disguised as observation about script structure

jokes.text: There's not much to the cat costume, but can't wait to show you what's underneath.
jokes.setup: Jeff texting as Britta to her supposed admirer
jokes.punchline: Sexually suggestive message that will go to her nephew
jokes.explanation: Setup for bigger cringe payoff, moderately funny inappropriate message

jokes.text: How could you think that was a good idea? I got a better question. How could it not be a great idea?
jokes.setup: Annie questioning Jeff's texting decision
jokes.punchline: Jeff's confident deflection showing he learned nothing
jokes.explanation: Jeff's arrogant inability to see obvious problems, typical character flaw

jokes.text: There's a rapist in the hallway. That's my landlord. And if he wanted to rape you, you'd be raped.
jokes.setup: Pierce warning about dangerous person in hallway
jokes.punchline: Annie's matter-of-fact correction about her intimidating landlord
jokes.explanation: Dark humor subverted into practical landlord information, good misdirection

jokes.text: What are you doing in an apartment above Dildopolis? And when did they open a second location?
jokes.setup: Pierce discovering Annie's living situation
jokes.punchline: Casually inquiring about sex shop expansion like a normal business
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce obliviousness treating sex shop like any retail chain, great escalation

jokes.text: I don't get it. Aren't everyone's parents rich?
jokes.setup: Pierce learning about Annie's financial struggles
jokes.punchline: Genuine confusion based on his privileged perspective
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Pierce's privilege bubble, economical and revealing

jokes.text: Every dollar from the Period Fairy when I was a kid. Excuse me. What's a Period Fairy?
jokes.setup: Annie mentioning Period Fairy savings casually
jokes.punchline: Pierce's confused inquiry about obviously made-up concept
jokes.explanation: Absurd childhood concept treated matter-of-factly, Pierce's innocent confusion

jokes.text: The fairy that gives you a dollar every time you get your period. Does she still come?
jokes.setup: Annie explaining the Period Fairy concept
jokes.punchline: Pierce's inappropriate follow-up question
jokes.explanation: Pierce's oblivious inappropriate question, good escalation of awkwardness

jokes.text: This crap hole is a monument to self-reliance. I found the drapes in a dumpster. Can you believe it? Yes.
jokes.setup: Pierce romanticizing Annie's terrible apartment
jokes.punchline: Annie's flat confirmation of Pierce's rhetorical question
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Pierce's romanticizing and Annie's reality, deadpan delivery

jokes.text: Because I was never here.
jokes.setup: Pierce explaining how money will appear
jokes.punchline: Dramatic exit line for simple money gift
jokes.explanation: Pierce's overly dramatic presentation of simple gesture, fits his theatrical nature

jokes.text: they told me the procedure for selling eggs takes three weeks. You save your eggs for a rainy day.
jokes.setup: Annie mentioning selling eggs for money
jokes.punchline: Pierce's literal advice about saving eggs
jokes.explanation: Pierce misunderstanding egg donation context, treating idiom literally

jokes.text: Hello. I'm Cornelius Hawthorne. One rainy day, my son Pierce and I were taking a stroll when I discovered the cleansing power of moist cotton, fresh from the field.
jokes.setup: Pierce performing his father's commercial
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic origin story for wet wipes
jokes.explanation: Absurd gravitas for mundane product, Pierce channeling family business pride

jokes.text: I wanna be in the commercial too, father. Well, then you should have done better in your damn audition.
jokes.setup: Pierce playing his younger self wanting to be in commercial
jokes.punchline: His father's harsh rejection in a family business context
jokes.explanation: Dark glimpse into Pierce's family dynamics, explains his need for validation

jokes.text: What's your name, son? Nigel. You're a good boy, Nigel. Your father is a lucky man.
jokes.setup: Pierce's father interacting with other child in commercial
jokes.punchline: Warm treatment of random child versus coldness to Pierce
jokes.explanation: Painful contrast showing Pierce's father's preference for other children

jokes.text: And who uses wite-out anymore?
jokes.setup: Pierce using correction fluid on script
jokes.punchline: Troy's practical question about outdated office supply
jokes.explanation: Simple generational observation, Troy's practical mindset

jokes.text: Hey, Crayon, do you know where I can get some drugs? I need them. Unfortunately, you can get them anywhere. Anywhere? I'm here! Zabba-da-zooey!
jokes.setup: Pierce's revised dialogue for the play
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical catchphrase 'Zabba-da-zooey!'
jokes.explanation: Pierce's attempt at memorable catchphrase, absurd but quotable nonsense

jokes.text: break a stinger! For the bees.
jokes.setup: Troy trying to say 'break a leg' theater tradition
jokes.punchline: Bee-themed variation with literal explanation
jokes.explanation: Cute costume-appropriate theater well-wish, Troy's earnest adaptation

jokes.text: By the way, is this your pen? I...think I put it back in my pocket after I wrote you that check yesterday.
jokes.setup: Pierce asking about pen ownership
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of giving Annie money
jokes.explanation: Pierce's manipulative reminder disguised as innocent question

jokes.text: Next time you think about drugs, think about baseball instead.
jokes.setup: Drug-free baseball giveaway
jokes.punchline: Absurd substitution therapy suggestion
jokes.explanation: Absurd anti-drug messaging, simple substitution logic

jokes.text: I actually thought he didn't even like me, but then he texted me out of nowhere
jokes.setup: Britta talking about her nephew's sudden interest
jokes.punchline: Unknowingly describing Jeff's inappropriate texts
jokes.explanation: Dramatic irony as Britta misinterprets Jeff's perverted texts as nephew's affection

jokes.text: You know that text we sent? It was to Britta's nephew. He sent her an emoti-penis.
jokes.setup: Jeff realizing the texting mistake
jokes.punchline: Troy's matter-of-fact description of inappropriate emoji
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe moment, Troy's casual coining of 'emoti-penis' term

jokes.text: Fine. Screw you, Abed. I can fix this.
jokes.setup: Jeff getting no response from Abed
jokes.punchline: Typical Jeff response to being ignored during crisis
jokes.explanation: Jeff's ego making him blame Abed for not helping with his problem

jokes.text: I'm drunk too. On lust. There's a picture. Oh! That's disappointing.
jokes.setup: Continued inappropriate responses from 14-year-old
jokes.punchline: Troy's underwhelmed reaction to explicit photo
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe with Troy's casual disappointment adding to absurdity

jokes.text: Are you ignoring me because I'm Korean? You're Chinese. Oh, there's a difference?
jokes.setup: Chang asking about being ignored for ethnicity
jokes.punchline: His confused response about Asian ethnicities
jokes.explanation: Chang's typical oblivious racism disguised as confusion, fits character perfectly

jokes.text: It's because you're crazy, all right? There's something wrong with you.
jokes.setup: Chang asking why he's being ignored
jokes.punchline: Shirley's blunt psychological assessment
jokes.explanation: Shirley breaking character to give honest assessment, refreshing directness

jokes.text: Jeff Winger is sexy even in a coffin. Mmm!
jokes.setup: Dean watching Jeff in funeral scene
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate attraction during death scene
jokes.explanation: Classic Dean inappropriate attraction, death setting makes it more uncomfortable

jokes.text: I know I'm green, but I feel blue.
jokes.setup: Jeff in green costume expressing sadness
jokes.punchline: Color pun connecting costume to emotion
jokes.explanation: Simple color pun, economical but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Look, it's Drugs with a crazy wig and sparklers. ♪ Pokemon ♪
jokes.setup: Pierce appearing with costume additions
jokes.punchline: Random Pokemon singing
jokes.explanation: Pierce's escalating performance chaos, random pop culture reference

jokes.text: Smells like something died in here.
jokes.setup: Pierce returning after being 'flushed'
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate death comment during funeral scene
jokes.explanation: Dark humor during funeral scene, Pierce's inappropriate timing

jokes.text: Being flushed doesn't necessarily mean you're dead. I mean, I could be a floater.
jokes.setup: Pierce justifying return after being flushed
jokes.punchline: Toilet humor explanation
jokes.explanation: Pierce's desperation to stay in show leads to gross explanation

jokes.text: You know what I think is a mixed message? Yesterday you couldn't pay your rent, and today you can.
jokes.setup: Annie complaining about mixed messages
jokes.punchline: Pierce's manipulative reminder of his financial help
jokes.explanation: Pierce weaponizing his generosity, revealing manipulation behind kindness

jokes.text: ♪ yi yi yi yi yi Bzzz! Getting rid of Drugs is a great I-bee-a.
jokes.setup: Jeff trying to salvage the show
jokes.punchline: Bee pun on 'idea'
jokes.explanation: Weak bee pun attempt during chaos, Jeff trying to maintain show

jokes.text: Listen to that! They're high on me! I'm a God! Where is that Nigel now?
jokes.setup: Kids chanting for Drugs character
jokes.punchline: Pierce's megalomaniacal response and childhood callback
jokes.explanation: Pierce's ego at peak inflation, callback to childhood trauma resolution through performance

jokes.text: You sick, sick, sad old man.
jokes.setup: Pierce threatening to withdraw support
jokes.punchline: Annie's brutal assessment of Pierce
jokes.explanation: Annie finally snapping with harsh but accurate description

jokes.text: Next time I'm at Dildopolis, I will not be coming upstairs to say hi.
jokes.setup: Pierce threatening to end their relationship
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to visiting sex shop below Annie's apartment
jokes.explanation: Great callback implying Pierce shops there regularly, adds layer to their relationship

jokes.text: We've got 50 at-risk preteens, armed with baseballs, chanting for Drugs
jokes.setup: Dean assessing the chaotic situation
jokes.punchline: Clinical description of absurd crisis
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of how badly things went wrong, highly quotable crisis assessment

jokes.text: Did someone say crazy person? No. Well, I heard it.
jokes.setup: Group discussing need for crazy person
jokes.punchline: Chang appearing and mishearing the conversation
jokes.explanation: Chang's perfect timing and self-awareness about being crazy

jokes.text: I'm gonna deep-fry your dog and eat your mama's face and I'm gonna wear your little brother's skin like pajamas.
jokes.setup: Chang as scary drugs character
jokes.punchline: Increasingly disturbing threats to children
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly disturbing escalation, Chang's unhinged creativity scaring kids effectively

jokes.text: Is that all you've got?
jokes.setup: Chang being attacked by children with baseballs
jokes.punchline: His defiant response while being beaten
jokes.explanation: Chang's commitment to the bit even while being assaulted by children

jokes.text: More disgusting than what you texted to an underage boy? Yeah, well, I didn't know you were you. And I didn't know you weren't my aunt.
jokes.setup: Jeff and Marcus confronting their texting situation
jokes.punchline: Both trying to claim victim status in inappropriate exchange
jokes.explanation: Mutual blame in disturbing situation, both trying to claim moral high ground

jokes.text: Okay, all right, let's just call this a draw. Okay, I'd be happy to, for a price.
jokes.setup: Jeff trying to end awkward situation peacefully
jokes.punchline: 14-year-old immediately trying to blackmail him
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation with kid immediately going to extortion, shows Jeff's worse problems ahead

jokes.text: Look, dude, she started it, okay? She's just as into me. More disgusting than what you texted to an underage boy? And I didn't know you weren't my aunt.
jokes.setup: Marcus defending himself to Jeff
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact response about not knowing Jeff wasn't his aunt
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe resolution - the kid being disturbingly logical about the situation

jokes.text: Why don't I go get Chang? You can tell him that yourself.
jokes.setup: Chang offering to bring Shirley to apologize to Chang
jokes.punchline: Shirley's sarcastic offer to facilitate impossible meeting
jokes.explanation: Shirley's sarcastic response to Chang's self-referential offer

jokes.text: It's okay. I did a lot of ad-libbing.
jokes.setup: Chang being congratulated after being beaten up
jokes.punchline: Treating improvised child-scaring as professional acting achievement
jokes.explanation: Chang's professional pride about traumatizing children, perfect understatement

jokes.text: By the way, your mom was the Period Fairy, right?
jokes.setup: Group hug and reconciliation moment
jokes.punchline: Pierce's inappropriate callback to private conversation
jokes.explanation: Pierce's inability to keep private information private, ruining tender moment

jokes.text: Annie, before you say anything, I just want you to know, even though I did nothing wrong, I'm still gonna give you money.
jokes.setup: Pierce preempting Annie's response
jokes.punchline: Claiming he did nothing wrong while offering compensation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce logic - completely self-serving while pretending to be generous

jokes.text: It's 2:00 A.M. Time for Dildopolis's night-owl deals.
jokes.setup: Late night commercial setting
jokes.punchline: Sex shop having late night sale promotions
jokes.explanation: Callback showing the store is real business with normal retail practices

jokes.text: All store purchases will show up on your credit card as ABC Dildos, incorporated.
jokes.setup: Privacy protection claim
jokes.punchline: Discrete billing is just as obvious as original name
jokes.explanation: Great irony about privacy protection being equally embarrassing, logical but absurd

tmdb_overview: Pierce's efforts to bribe director Annie may undermine the theater group's antidrug play; Jeff's texting prank puts Britta in an awkward situation.
insight: Drug-episode earnestness undercuts joke density; cringe beats setup in uneven 73-point effort.
